    88→  title Component Diagram -- Reactive Layer Internals
    89→
    90→  Container_Boundary(reactive, "Reactive Layer") {
    91→    Component(liveSelectMgr, "Live Select Manager", "Manages LIVE SELECT subscriptions per workspace per table")
    92→    Component(classifier, "Event Classifier", "Deterministic rules engine: entity type + transition + severity -> notification targets")
    93→    Component(feedBridge, "Feed SSE Bridge", "Transforms classified events to GovernanceFeedItem, batches within 500ms window")
    94→    Component(coordinator, "Agent Coordinator", "Resolves affected agents via graph traversal, writes context_queue")
    95→    Component(dampener, "Loop Dampener", "Sliding window counter: per-entity, per-source, per-workspace")
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB")
    99→  Container(sseReg, "SSE Registry")
   100→  Container(ctxQueue, "context_queue table")
   101→
   102→  Rel(surreal, liveSelectMgr, "Delivers LIVE SELECT events", "WebSocket")
   103→  Rel(liveSelectMgr, classifier, "Forwards raw graph events")
   104→  Rel(classifier, feedBridge, "Sends classified events (level=log for feed)")
   105→  Rel(classifier, coordinator, "Sends classified events (level=enqueue|interrupt)")
   106→  Rel(coordinator, dampener, "Checks dampening before processing")
   107→  Rel(coordinator, surreal, "Traverses dependency graph")
   108→  Rel(coordinator, ctxQueue, "Writes pending context updates")
   109→  Rel(feedBridge, sseReg, "Emits batched feed_update SSE events")
   110→  Rel(dampener, surreal, "Creates meta-observation on activation")
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Component Boundaries
   114→
   115→### New Modules
   116→
   117→| Module | Path | Responsibility |
   118→|--------|------|---------------|
   119→| Live Select Manager | `app/src/server/reactive/live-select-manager.ts` | Create/manage LIVE SELECT subscriptions per workspace. Requires dedicated WebSocket Surreal connection (separate from query connection). |
   120→| Event Classifier | `app/src/server/reactive/event-classifier.ts` | Pure function: graph event -> array of `{ target, level }`. Deterministic rules from shared-artifacts-registry classification table. |
   121→| Feed SSE Bridge | `app/src/server/reactive/feed-sse-bridge.ts` | Subscribes to classified events, transforms to `GovernanceFeedItem`, batches within 500ms window, pushes via SSE registry. Manages per-workspace SSE streams. |
   122→| Agent Coordinator | `app/src/server/reactive/agent-coordinator.ts` | Always-on service. Receives classified events, resolves affected agents via graph traversal, writes to `context_queue`. Owns loop dampener state. |
   123→| Loop Dampener | `app/src/server/reactive/loop-dampener.ts` | Pure function + state container: sliding window event counter. Threshold check returns dampen/allow. |
   124→
   125→### Extended Existing Modules
   126→
   127→| Module | Path | Change |
   128→|--------|------|--------|
   129→| SSE Registry | `app/src/server/streaming/sse-registry.ts` | Add per-workspace stream management (current: per-message only). New methods: `registerWorkspaceStream`, `emitWorkspaceEvent`, `handleWorkspaceStreamRequest`. |
   130→| Context Injector | `app/src/server/proxy/context-injector.ts` | Add `buildQueueContextXml()` pure function. New XML blocks: `<urgent-context>`, `<context-update>`. |
   131→| Anthropic Proxy Route | `app/src/server/proxy/anthropic-proxy-route.ts` | Wire `loadPendingContextUpdates()` into context injection pipeline (Step 5). Mark delivered items after injection. |
   132→| Start Server | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Start Agent Coordinator as always-on service. Register feed stream SSE endpoint. Create dedicated WS Surreal connection for LIVE SELECT. |
   133→| Server Dependencies | `app/src/server/runtime/types.ts` | Add `reactiveSurreal: Surreal` (dedicated WS connection for LIVE SELECT). |
   134→| MCP Route | `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` | Extend context endpoint to include `urgent_updates` and `context_updates` arrays from `context_queue`. |
   135→
   136→## Technology Stack
   137→
   138→| Technology | Purpose | License | Rationale |
   139→|------------|---------|---------|-----------|
   140→| SurrealDB LIVE SELECT | Push-based graph change notifications | BSL 1.1 | Already in stack. Native to DB. No additional dependency. |
   141→| SurrealDB JS SDK v2 | LIVE SELECT subscription API | MIT | Already in stack (`surrealdb` package). Provides `surreal.live()` method. |
   142→| Bun ReadableStream | SSE transport | MIT | Already in stack. Used by existing SSE registry. |
   143→| EventSource (browser) | Client SSE consumption | Web standard | Already used by orchestrator stream client. |
   144→
   145→No new dependencies required. All components use existing stack.
   146→
   147→## Integration Patterns
   148→
   149→### 1. LIVE SELECT Subscription
   150→
   151→The Live Select Manager creates a **dedicated Surreal WebSocket connection** (`reactiveSurreal`) separate from the query connection. This prevents LIVE SELECT traffic from interfering with request-scoped queries.
   152→
   153→**Subscribed tables** (workspace-scoped):
   154→- `decision` -- status transitions (confirmed, superseded)
   155→- `task` -- status transitions (blocked, done, in_progress)
   156→- `observation` -- creation (all severities)
   157→- `question` -- creation (high priority)
   158→- `suggestion` -- creation
   159→- `learning` -- creation (pending approval)
   160→- `agent_session` -- status transitions (ended, error)
   161→
   162→**Excluded tables** (too high volume):
   163→- `trace` -- uses existing DEFINE EVENT webhooks
   164→- `message` -- not relevant for governance feed
   165→- `extracted_from` -- relation table, high write volume
   166→
   167→**SurrealDB v3.0 LIVE SELECT constraint**: WHERE clauses do not support bound parameters. Workspace filtering must happen application-side after receiving the event, not in the LIVE SELECT query itself.
   168→
   169→### 2. Event Classification Flow
   170→
   171→```
   172→LIVE SELECT event
   173→  |
   174→  v
   175→Event Classifier (pure, deterministic)
   176→  |
   177→  +---> Feed targets: { target: "feed", level: "log" }
   178→  |
   179→  +---> Agent targets: { target: RecordId<agent_session>, level: "enqueue" | "interrupt" }
   180→  |     (requires graph traversal to resolve affected sessions)
   181→  |
   182→  +---> Observer target: { target: "observer", level: "log" }
   183→```
   184→
   185→Classification rules are defined in the shared-artifacts-registry and encoded as a lookup table in the classifier module.
   186→
   187→### 3. Feed SSE Bridge -> Client
   188→
   189→```
   190→Classified event (level=log, target=feed)
   191→  |
   192→  v
   193→Feed SSE Bridge
   194→  |-- Transforms to GovernanceFeedItem (same contract as GET endpoint)
   195→  |-- Batches within 500ms window (prevents burst flooding)
   196→  |-- Assigns monotonic event ID
   197→  |
   198→  v
   199→SSE Registry (per-workspace stream)
   200→  |
   201→  v
   202→EventSource (browser)
   203→  |-- Merges into client-side feed state
   204→  |-- Deduplicates by item.id
   205→  |-- Updates tier counts
   206→```
   207→
   208→**SSE event format**:
   209→```
   210→id: <monotonic-event-id>
   211→event: feed_update
   212→data: { "items": GovernanceFeedItem[], "removals": string[] }
   213→```
   214→
   215→`removals` contains IDs of items that moved tiers or were resolved (e.g., decision confirmed removes it from blocking tier).
   216→
   217→### 4. Coordinator -> context_queue -> Proxy
   218→
   219→```
   220→Classified event (level=enqueue|interrupt, target=agent_session:xyz)
   221→  |
   222→  v
   223→Agent Coordinator
   224→  |-- Checks loop dampener (skip if dampened)
   225→  |-- Resolves affected sessions via graph traversal
   226→  |-- Builds context update payload
   227→  |
   228→  v
   229→INSERT into context_queue (session, level, payload, created_at)
   230→  |
   231→  ... time passes (agent is mid-generation) ...
   232→  |
   233→  v
   234→LLM Proxy receives next API call for session xyz
   235→  |-- Context Injector queries: SELECT * FROM context_queue
   236→  |   WHERE session = $sess AND delivered_at IS NONE
   237→  |   AND created_at > time::now() - 30m
   238→  |   ORDER BY level DESC, created_at ASC
   239→  |
   240→  |-- Injects: interrupt items -> <urgent-context> (before <brain-context>)
   241→  |             enqueue items -> <context-update> (after <brain-context>)
   242→  |
   243→  |-- Marks delivered: UPDATE context_queue SET delivered_at = time::now()
   244→  |   WHERE id IN $delivered_ids (fire-and-forget via inflight.track)
   245→```
   246→
   247→### 5. Reconnection Protocol
   248→
   249→| Disconnection Duration | Strategy |
   250→|------------------------|----------|
   251→| < 10 minutes | Delta sync: replay events from `Last-Event-ID` |
   252→| >= 10 minutes | Full refresh: trigger GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed reload |
   253→
   254→Server maintains a bounded event buffer (last 1000 events per workspace, ~10 minutes at typical volume) for delta replay.
   255→
   256→## `context_queue` Table Schema
   257→
   258→```sql
   259→DEFINE TABLE context_queue SCHEMAFULL;
   260→DEFINE FIELD session ON context_queue TYPE record<agent_session>;
   261→DEFINE FIELD level ON context_queue TYPE string
   262→  ASSERT $value IN ["enqueue", "interrupt"];
   263→DEFINE FIELD payload ON context_queue TYPE object;
   264→DEFINE FIELD payload.entity_table ON context_queue TYPE string;
   265→DEFINE FIELD payload.entity_id ON context_queue TYPE string;
   266→DEFINE FIELD payload.change_type ON context_queue TYPE string;
   267→DEFINE FIELD payload.description ON context_queue TYPE string;
   268→DEFINE FIELD payload.impact ON context_queue TYPE string;
   269→DEFINE FIELD payload.recommendation ON context_queue TYPE option<string>;
   270→DEFINE FIELD payload.superseded_by ON context_queue TYPE option<string>;
   271→DEFINE FIELD workspace ON context_queue TYPE record<workspace>;
   272→DEFINE FIELD source_event_table ON context_queue TYPE string;
   273→DEFINE FIELD source_event_id ON context_queue TYPE string;
   274→DEFINE FIELD created_at ON context_queue TYPE datetime DEFAULT time::now();
   275→DEFINE FIELD delivered_at ON context_queue TYPE option<datetime>;
   276→DEFINE INDEX idx_context_queue_session ON context_queue FIELDS session;
   277→DEFINE INDEX idx_context_queue_pending ON context_queue FIELDS session, delivered_at;
   278→```
   279→
   280→**TTL enforcement**: The proxy query filters `created_at > time::now() - 30m`. A periodic cleanup job (every 10 minutes) deletes rows older than 1 hour: `DELETE FROM context_queue WHERE created_at < time::now() - 1h;`
   281→
   282→## Deployment Architecture
   283→
   284→No new services. All components run in-process within the existing Bun server:
   285→
   286→- **Live Select Manager**: Started in `startServer()` after Surreal connections are established. Creates a dedicated `reactiveSurreal` WebSocket connection for LIVE SELECT subscriptions.
   287→- **Agent Coordinator**: Started in `startServer()` as an always-on event listener. Receives events from Live Select Manager, writes to `context_queue`.
   288→- **Feed SSE Bridge**: Started per-workspace on first SSE connection. Stopped when last client disconnects (with 30s grace period).
   289→- **Loop Dampener**: In-memory state owned by the Coordinator instance. State is per-workspace, per-entity, per-source-agent. Not persisted -- resets on server restart (acceptable: dampening is short-lived).
   290→
   291→### Dedicated Surreal Connection
   292→
   293→```
   294→Brain Server
   295→  |
   296→  +-- surreal (existing): HTTP/WS for request-scoped queries
   297→  |
   298→  +-- reactiveSurreal (NEW): WS-only for LIVE SELECT subscriptions
   299→  |   - Separate connection prevents LIVE SELECT traffic from
   300→  |     blocking request-scoped queries
   301→  |   - Auto-reconnects on disconnect (Surreal SDK handles this)
   302→  |   - Subscribes to tables listed in "Subscribed tables" above
   303→```
   304→
   305→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   306→
   307→### Performance
   308→- Event classification: < 100ms (pure function, no IO for initial classification; graph traversal for agent resolution adds up to 200ms)
   309→- Feed SSE delivery: < 2 seconds from graph write (95th percentile)
   310→- 500ms batching window prevents client-side event flooding
   311→- Dedicated Surreal connection prevents LIVE SELECT from blocking queries
   312→
   313→### Reliability
   314→- SSE keep-alive every 15 seconds prevents connection timeout
   315→- Delta sync on reconnection (< 10 min gap)
   316→- Full refresh on extended disconnection (>= 10 min gap)
   317→- Loop dampener prevents cascading notification storms
   318→- Coordinator is fail-safe: if it crashes, agents still work (they just don't get mid-session updates)
   319→
   320→### Maintainability
   321→- Event classifier is a pure function with lookup table -- easy to test and extend
   322→- Feed SSE Bridge reuses existing `GovernanceFeedItem` contract -- no schema drift
   323→- `context_queue` table provides clean boundary between always-on coordinator and request-scoped proxy
   324→- All new modules in `app/src/server/reactive/` -- clear module boundary
   325→
   326→### Security
   327→- Feed SSE endpoint (`/api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream`) scoped to workspace ID in URL path -- same access model as existing GET feed endpoint
   328→- `context_queue` reads are scoped by `session` field (proxy already resolves session from authenticated request)
   329→- LIVE SELECT events are filtered application-side by workspace (SurrealDB v3.0 LIVE SELECT does not support WHERE with bound params) -- classifier discards events for non-matching workspaces before any processing
   330→- No new authentication mechanisms -- all new endpoints use existing auth middleware
   331→
   332→### Observability
   333→- Log every LIVE SELECT subscription start/stop
   334→- Log every classification decision (with entity, level, targets)
   335→- Log every context_queue write and delivery
   336→- Log loop dampener activations (+ meta-observation in graph)
   337→- SSE connection status tracked per workspace
   338→
   339→## Migration Path
   340→
   341→### Phase 3: Foundation (US-GRC-01 + US-GRC-02)
   342→1. Migration: (none -- no new tables yet in Phase 3; SSE bridge uses in-memory event buffer)
   343→2. Create `reactiveSurreal` dedicated WS connection in `dependencies.ts`
   344→3. Build Live Select Manager with workspace-scoped subscriptions
   345→4. Build Event Classifier (pure function)
   346→5. Build Feed SSE Bridge with batching
   347→6. Extend SSE Registry for per-workspace streams
   348→7. Register SSE endpoint: `GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream`
   349→
   350→### Phase 4: Coordinator (US-GRC-03)
   351→1. Migration `0053_context_queue.surql`: Create `context_queue` table
   352→2. Build Agent Coordinator (always-on service)
   353→3. Build Loop Dampener
   354→4. Start Coordinator in `startServer()`
   355→
   356→### Phase 5: Delivery (US-GRC-04)
   357→1. Extend `context-injector.ts` with `buildQueueContextXml()`
   358→2. Wire queue loading into proxy pipeline
   359→3. Extend MCP context endpoint with queue items
   360→4. Emit feed event on interrupt delivery
   361→

# Graph-Reactive Agent Coordination -- Architecture

## System Context

Brain is a knowledge graph platform where agents coordinate through shared state in SurrealDB. Today, the governance feed is poll-on-load (14 parallel queries per page load), agents discover context changes only at session start, and conflict detection relies on periodic Observer scans.

This feature replaces poll-based reactivity with push-based coordination powered by SurrealDB LIVE SELECT, enabling:
- Real-time governance feed updates via SSE
- Automatic agent context injection when graph state changes mid-session
- Immediate conflict notification to affected agents

## Confirmed Architecture Decisions (Pre-Existing)

These are NOT open for re-decision:

1. SurrealDB LIVE SELECT as event source (push-based, native to stack)
2. Three notification levels: interrupt, enqueue, log
3. Coordinator writes to `context_queue`, proxy reads from it
4. LLM proxy delivers via `<urgent-context>` and `<context-update>` XML blocks
5. Never cancel current generation -- interrupts wait for next turn
6. Loop dampening: >3 events on same entity from same source in 60s
7. Deterministic event classifier (no LLM in classification path)
8. WebSocket transport already in use for SurrealDB connection

## C4 System Context (L1)

```mermaid
C4Context
  title System Context -- Graph-Reactive Agent Coordination

  Person(admin, "Workspace Admin", "Monitors agent activity via governance feed")
  Person(coder, "Coding Agent (MCP)", "Works on tasks via LLM proxy")

  System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph + agent coordination")

  System_Ext(surrealdb, "SurrealDB", "Graph database with LIVE SELECT")
  System_Ext(anthropic, "Anthropic API", "LLM provider")

  Rel(admin, brain, "Views live feed via SSE")
  Rel(coder, brain, "Sends LLM requests via proxy")
  Rel(brain, surrealdb, "Subscribes to graph changes via LIVE SELECT")
  Rel(brain, surrealdb, "Reads/writes graph state via queries")
  Rel(brain, anthropic, "Forwards LLM requests with injected context")
```

## C4 Container (L2)

```mermaid
C4Container
  title Container Diagram -- Graph-Reactive Coordination

  Person(admin, "Workspace Admin")
  Person(agent, "Coding Agent")

  Container_Boundary(brain, "Brain Server (Bun)") {
    Container(feedRoute, "Feed Route", "GET handler", "Serves initial feed state via 14 parallel queries")
    Container(feedSseBridge, "Feed SSE Bridge", "NEW", "Subscribes LIVE SELECT, transforms to GovernanceFeedItem, pushes via SSE")
    Container(eventClassifier, "Event Classifier", "NEW", "Deterministic rules: graph event -> notification level per target")
    Container(coordinator, "Agent Coordinator", "NEW", "Always-on: resolves affected agents, writes to context_queue")
    Container(proxyRoute, "LLM Proxy Route", "Existing", "Forwards LLM requests with context injection")
    Container(contextInjector, "Context Injector", "Existing+Extended", "Builds brain-context XML, NEW: reads context_queue")
    Container(sseRegistry, "SSE Registry", "Existing+Extended", "Manages SSE streams, NEW: per-workspace feed streams")
    Container(sessionLifecycle, "Session Lifecycle", "Existing", "Agent session management")
  }

  ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "Graph DB", "LIVE SELECT subscriptions + context_queue table")

  Rel(admin, feedRoute, "Loads initial feed", "GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed")
  Rel(admin, feedSseBridge, "Streams updates", "GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream (SSE)")
  Rel(agent, proxyRoute, "Sends LLM requests", "POST /proxy/llm/anthropic/v1/messages")
  Rel(proxyRoute, contextInjector, "Injects brain-context + queue updates")
  Rel(surreal, feedSseBridge, "Pushes graph changes", "LIVE SELECT")
  Rel(surreal, coordinator, "Pushes graph changes", "LIVE SELECT")
  Rel(feedSseBridge, eventClassifier, "Classifies events")
  Rel(coordinator, eventClassifier, "Classifies events")
  Rel(coordinator, surreal, "Writes context updates", "INSERT into context_queue")
  Rel(coordinator, surreal, "Traverses graph", "depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to")
  Rel(contextInjector, surreal, "Reads pending updates", "SELECT from context_queue")
  Rel(feedSseBridge, sseRegistry, "Emits feed events")
```

## C4 Component (L3) -- Reactive Layer

The reactive layer is complex enough (5+ internal components) to warrant L3 detail.

```mermaid
C4Component
  title Component Diagram -- Reactive Layer Internals

  Container_Boundary(reactive, "Reactive Layer") {
    Component(liveSelectMgr, "Live Select Manager", "Manages LIVE SELECT subscriptions per workspace per table")
    Component(classifier, "Event Classifier", "Deterministic rules engine: entity type + transition + severity -> notification targets")
    Component(feedBridge, "Feed SSE Bridge", "Transforms classified events to GovernanceFeedItem, batches within 500ms window")
    Component(coordinator, "Agent Coordinator", "Resolves affected agents via graph traversal, writes context_queue")
    Component(dampener, "Loop Dampener", "Sliding window counter: per-entity, per-source, per-workspace")
  }

  ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB")
  Container(sseReg, "SSE Registry")
  Container(ctxQueue, "context_queue table")

  Rel(surreal, liveSelectMgr, "Delivers LIVE SELECT events", "WebSocket")
  Rel(liveSelectMgr, classifier, "Forwards raw graph events")
  Rel(classifier, feedBridge, "Sends classified events (level=log for feed)")
  Rel(classifier, coordinator, "Sends classified events (level=enqueue|interrupt)")
  Rel(coordinator, dampener, "Checks dampening before processing")
  Rel(coordinator, surreal, "Traverses dependency graph")
  Rel(coordinator, ctxQueue, "Writes pending context updates")
  Rel(feedBridge, sseReg, "Emits batched feed_update SSE events")
  Rel(dampener, surreal, "Creates meta-observation on activation")
```

## Component Boundaries

### New Modules

| Module | Path | Responsibility |
|--------|------|---------------|
| Live Select Manager | `app/src/server/reactive/live-select-manager.ts` | Create/manage LIVE SELECT subscriptions per workspace. Requires dedicated WebSocket Surreal connection (separate from query connection). |
| Event Classifier | `app/src/server/reactive/event-classifier.ts` | Pure function: graph event -> array of `{ target, level }`. Deterministic rules from shared-artifacts-registry classification table. |
| Feed SSE Bridge | `app/src/server/reactive/feed-sse-bridge.ts` | Subscribes to classified events, transforms to `GovernanceFeedItem`, batches within 500ms window, pushes via SSE registry. Manages per-workspace SSE streams. |
| Agent Coordinator | `app/src/server/reactive/agent-coordinator.ts` | Always-on service. Receives classified events, resolves affected agents via graph traversal, writes to `context_queue`. Owns loop dampener state. |
| Loop Dampener | `app/src/server/reactive/loop-dampener.ts` | Pure function + state container: sliding window event counter. Threshold check returns dampen/allow. |

### Extended Existing Modules

| Module | Path | Change |
|--------|------|--------|
| SSE Registry | `app/src/server/streaming/sse-registry.ts` | Add per-workspace stream management (current: per-message only). New methods: `registerWorkspaceStream`, `emitWorkspaceEvent`, `handleWorkspaceStreamRequest`. |
| Context Injector | `app/src/server/proxy/context-injector.ts` | Add `buildQueueContextXml()` pure function. New XML blocks: `<urgent-context>`, `<context-update>`. |
| Anthropic Proxy Route | `app/src/server/proxy/anthropic-proxy-route.ts` | Wire `loadPendingContextUpdates()` into context injection pipeline (Step 5). Mark delivered items after injection. |
| Start Server | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Start Agent Coordinator as always-on service. Register feed stream SSE endpoint. Create dedicated WS Surreal connection for LIVE SELECT. |
| Server Dependencies | `app/src/server/runtime/types.ts` | Add `reactiveSurreal: Surreal` (dedicated WS connection for LIVE SELECT). |
| MCP Route | `app/src/server/mcp/mcp-route.ts` | Extend context endpoint to include `urgent_updates` and `context_updates` arrays from `context_queue`. |

## Technology Stack

| Technology | Purpose | License | Rationale |
|------------|---------|---------|-----------|
| SurrealDB LIVE SELECT | Push-based graph change notifications | BSL 1.1 | Already in stack. Native to DB. No additional dependency. |
| SurrealDB JS SDK v2 | LIVE SELECT subscription API | MIT | Already in stack (`surrealdb` package). Provides `surreal.live()` method. |
| Bun ReadableStream | SSE transport | MIT | Already in stack. Used by existing SSE registry. |
| EventSource (browser) | Client SSE consumption | Web standard | Already used by orchestrator stream client. |

No new dependencies required. All components use existing stack.

## Integration Patterns

### 1. LIVE SELECT Subscription

The Live Select Manager creates a **dedicated Surreal WebSocket connection** (`reactiveSurreal`) separate from the query connection. This prevents LIVE SELECT traffic from interfering with request-scoped queries.

**Subscribed tables** (workspace-scoped):
- `decision` -- status transitions (confirmed, superseded)
- `task` -- status transitions (blocked, done, in_progress)
- `observation` -- creation (all severities)
- `question` -- creation (high priority)
- `suggestion` -- creation
- `learning` -- creation (pending approval)
- `agent_session` -- status transitions (ended, error)

**Excluded tables** (too high volume):
- `trace` -- uses existing DEFINE EVENT webhooks
- `message` -- not relevant for governance feed
- `extracted_from` -- relation table, high write volume

**SurrealDB v3.0 LIVE SELECT constraint**: WHERE clauses do not support bound parameters. Workspace filtering must happen application-side after receiving the event, not in the LIVE SELECT query itself.

### 2. Event Classification Flow

```
LIVE SELECT event
  |
  v
Event Classifier (pure, deterministic)
  |
  +---> Feed targets: { target: "feed", level: "log" }
  |
  +---> Agent targets: { target: RecordId<agent_session>, level: "enqueue" | "interrupt" }
  |     (requires graph traversal to resolve affected sessions)
  |
  +---> Observer target: { target: "observer", level: "log" }
```

Classification rules are defined in the shared-artifacts-registry and encoded as a lookup table in the classifier module.

### 3. Feed SSE Bridge -> Client

```
Classified event (level=log, target=feed)
  |
  v
Feed SSE Bridge
  |-- Transforms to GovernanceFeedItem (same contract as GET endpoint)
  |-- Batches within 500ms window (prevents burst flooding)
  |-- Assigns monotonic event ID
  |
  v
SSE Registry (per-workspace stream)
  |
  v
EventSource (browser)
  |-- Merges into client-side feed state
  |-- Deduplicates by item.id
  |-- Updates tier counts
```

**SSE event format**:
```
id: <monotonic-event-id>
event: feed_update
data: { "items": GovernanceFeedItem[], "removals": string[] }
```

`removals` contains IDs of items that moved tiers or were resolved (e.g., decision confirmed removes it from blocking tier).

### 4. Coordinator -> context_queue -> Proxy

```
Classified event (level=enqueue|interrupt, target=agent_session:xyz)
  |
  v
Agent Coordinator
  |-- Checks loop dampener (skip if dampened)
  |-- Resolves affected sessions via graph traversal
  |-- Builds context update payload
  |
  v
INSERT into context_queue (session, level, payload, created_at)
  |
  ... time passes (agent is mid-generation) ...
  |
  v
LLM Proxy receives next API call for session xyz
  |-- Context Injector queries: SELECT * FROM context_queue
  |   WHERE session = $sess AND delivered_at IS NONE
  |   AND created_at > time::now() - 30m
  |   ORDER BY level DESC, created_at ASC
  |
  |-- Injects: interrupt items -> <urgent-context> (before <brain-context>)
  |             enqueue items -> <context-update> (after <brain-context>)
  |
  |-- Marks delivered: UPDATE context_queue SET delivered_at = time::now()
  |   WHERE id IN $delivered_ids (fire-and-forget via inflight.track)
```

### 5. Reconnection Protocol

| Disconnection Duration | Strategy |
|------------------------|----------|
| < 10 minutes | Delta sync: replay events from `Last-Event-ID` |
| >= 10 minutes | Full refresh: trigger GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed reload |

Server maintains a bounded event buffer (last 1000 events per workspace, ~10 minutes at typical volume) for delta replay.

## `context_queue` Table Schema

```sql
DEFINE TABLE context_queue SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE FIELD session ON context_queue TYPE record<agent_session>;
DEFINE FIELD level ON context_queue TYPE string
  ASSERT $value IN ["enqueue", "interrupt"];
DEFINE FIELD payload ON context_queue TYPE object;
DEFINE FIELD payload.entity_table ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD payload.entity_id ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD payload.change_type ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD payload.description ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD payload.impact ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD payload.recommendation ON context_queue TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD payload.superseded_by ON context_queue TYPE option<string>;
DEFINE FIELD workspace ON context_queue TYPE record<workspace>;
DEFINE FIELD source_event_table ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD source_event_id ON context_queue TYPE string;
DEFINE FIELD created_at ON context_queue TYPE datetime DEFAULT time::now();
DEFINE FIELD delivered_at ON context_queue TYPE option<datetime>;
DEFINE INDEX idx_context_queue_session ON context_queue FIELDS session;
DEFINE INDEX idx_context_queue_pending ON context_queue FIELDS session, delivered_at;
```

**TTL enforcement**: The proxy query filters `created_at > time::now() - 30m`. A periodic cleanup job (every 10 minutes) deletes rows older than 1 hour: `DELETE FROM context_queue WHERE created_at < time::now() - 1h;`

## Deployment Architecture

No new services. All components run in-process within the existing Bun server:

- **Live Select Manager**: Started in `startServer()` after Surreal connections are established. Creates a dedicated `reactiveSurreal` WebSocket connection for LIVE SELECT subscriptions.
- **Agent Coordinator**: Started in `startServer()` as an always-on event listener. Receives events from Live Select Manager, writes to `context_queue`.
- **Feed SSE Bridge**: Started per-workspace on first SSE connection. Stopped when last client disconnects (with 30s grace period).
- **Loop Dampener**: In-memory state owned by the Coordinator instance. State is per-workspace, per-entity, per-source-agent. Not persisted -- resets on server restart (acceptable: dampening is short-lived).

### Dedicated Surreal Connection

```
Brain Server
  |
  +-- surreal (existing): HTTP/WS for request-scoped queries
  |
  +-- reactiveSurreal (NEW): WS-only for LIVE SELECT subscriptions
  |   - Separate connection prevents LIVE SELECT traffic from
  |     blocking request-scoped queries
  |   - Auto-reconnects on disconnect (Surreal SDK handles this)
  |   - Subscribes to tables listed in "Subscribed tables" above
```

## Quality Attribute Strategies

### Performance
- Event classification: < 100ms (pure function, no IO for initial classification; graph traversal for agent resolution adds up to 200ms)
- Feed SSE delivery: < 2 seconds from graph write (95th percentile)
- 500ms batching window prevents client-side event flooding
- Dedicated Surreal connection prevents LIVE SELECT from blocking queries

### Reliability
- SSE keep-alive every 15 seconds prevents connection timeout
- Delta sync on reconnection (< 10 min gap)
- Full refresh on extended disconnection (>= 10 min gap)
- Loop dampener prevents cascading notification storms
- Coordinator is fail-safe: if it crashes, agents still work (they just don't get mid-session updates)

### Maintainability
- Event classifier is a pure function with lookup table -- easy to test and extend
- Feed SSE Bridge reuses existing `GovernanceFeedItem` contract -- no schema drift
- `context_queue` table provides clean boundary between always-on coordinator and request-scoped proxy
- All new modules in `app/src/server/reactive/` -- clear module boundary

### Security
- Feed SSE endpoint (`/api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream`) scoped to workspace ID in URL path -- same access model as existing GET feed endpoint
- `context_queue` reads are scoped by `session` field (proxy already resolves session from authenticated request)
- LIVE SELECT events are filtered application-side by workspace (SurrealDB v3.0 LIVE SELECT does not support WHERE with bound params) -- classifier discards events for non-matching workspaces before any processing
- No new authentication mechanisms -- all new endpoints use existing auth middleware

### Observability
- Log every LIVE SELECT subscription start/stop
- Log every classification decision (with entity, level, targets)
- Log every context_queue write and delivery
- Log loop dampener activations (+ meta-observation in graph)
- SSE connection status tracked per workspace

## Migration Path

### Phase 3: Foundation (US-GRC-01 + US-GRC-02)
1. Migration: (none -- no new tables yet in Phase 3; SSE bridge uses in-memory event buffer)
2. Create `reactiveSurreal` dedicated WS connection in `dependencies.ts`
3. Build Live Select Manager with workspace-scoped subscriptions
4. Build Event Classifier (pure function)
5. Build Feed SSE Bridge with batching
6. Extend SSE Registry for per-workspace streams
7. Register SSE endpoint: `GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream`

### Phase 4: Coordinator (US-GRC-03)
1. Migration `0053_context_queue.surql`: Create `context_queue` table
2. Build Agent Coordinator (always-on service)
3. Build Loop Dampener
4. Start Coordinator in `startServer()`

### Phase 5: Delivery (US-GRC-04)
1. Extend `context-injector.ts` with `buildQueueContextXml()`
2. Wire queue loading into proxy pipeline
3. Extend MCP context endpoint with queue items
4. Emit feed event on interrupt delivery

# Graph-Reactive Agent Coordination -- Requirements Index

## Feature Overview

Replace Brain's poll-based reactivity with a reactive layer powered by SurrealDB LIVE SELECT, enabling real-time governance feed updates, automatic agent context injection, and immediate conflict detection.

## Phasing

| Phase | Scope | Stories |
|-------|-------|---------|
| **Phase 3: Foundation** | LIVE SELECT -> SSE bridge for UI feed reactivity + event classification | US-GRC-01, US-GRC-02 |
| **Phase 4: Coordinator** | Always-on service: LIVE SELECT listener → graph traversal → write to `context_queue` table | US-GRC-03 |
| **Phase 5: Delivery** | LLM proxy reads `context_queue` during context injection, delivers to agents on next API call | US-GRC-04 |

## Dependency Graph

```
US-GRC-01 (Live Feed SSE Bridge) ---+
    |                                |
    v                                v
US-GRC-02 (Event Classifier) -------+
    |
    v
US-GRC-03 (Agent Coordinator + Loop Dampening)
    |
    v
US-GRC-04 (Interrupt Context Injection)
```

## Stories

| ID | Title | Type | Phase | Est. Days | Scenarios | Status |
|----|-------|------|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| US-GRC-01 | Live Governance Feed via SSE | Story | 3 | 3 | 6 | Draft |
| US-GRC-02 | Graph Event Classifier | Story | 3 | 2 | 6 | Draft |
| US-GRC-03 | Agent Coordinator with Context Injection | Story | 4 | 3 | 7 | Draft |
| US-GRC-04 | Proxy Context Queue Delivery | Story | 5 | 1 | 5 | Draft |

## JTBD Traceability

| Job Story | Stories |
|-----------|---------|
| JS-GRC-01: Real-Time Governance Awareness | US-GRC-01, US-GRC-02 |
| JS-GRC-02: Reactive Agent Wake-Up | US-GRC-03, US-GRC-04 |
| JS-GRC-03: Real-Time Conflict Detection | US-GRC-02, US-GRC-03, US-GRC-04 |
| JS-GRC-04: MCP Context Freshness | US-GRC-03 (enqueue mechanism + context endpoint) |

## Opportunity Score Alignment

| Outcome (Score) | Primary Story |
|-----------------|---------------|
| Agent stale-context prevention (17.4) | US-GRC-03 |
| Feed real-time updates (16.5) | US-GRC-01 |
| Manual refresh elimination (16.0) | US-GRC-01 |
| Notification urgency classification (14.9) | US-GRC-02 |
| Conflict notification latency (14.8) | US-GRC-04 |

## Personas

| Persona | Primary Stories |
|---------|----------------|
| Marcus Oliveira (Workspace Admin) | US-GRC-01, US-GRC-02, US-GRC-03 |
| Chat Agent (Orchestrator) | US-GRC-03, US-GRC-04 |
| Observer Agent | US-GRC-02 (triggers), US-GRC-03 (receives) |
| Tomas Chen (MCP/CLI user) | US-GRC-03 (context freshness) |

## Key Risks

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation | Story |
|------|----------|------------|-------|
| SSE events + GET feed items schema mismatch | HIGH | Share GovernanceFeedItem type | US-GRC-01 |
| Agent coordination loops from cascading events | HIGH | Loop dampening with meta-observation | US-GRC-03 |
| High-volume tables (trace) flood LIVE SELECT | HIGH | Exclude from LIVE SELECT; use DEFINE EVENT | US-GRC-01 |
| Context injection confuses agent mid-task | MEDIUM | Clear framing, never cancel current generation | US-GRC-04 |
| False-positive conflict interrupts | MEDIUM | Confidence threshold before interrupt | US-GRC-02 |

## UX Artifacts

All in `docs/ux/graph-reactive-coordination/`:
- `jtbd-analysis.md` -- 4 job stories, forces analysis, opportunity scores, personas
- `journey-reactive-feed-visual.md` -- feed journey with ASCII mockups
- `journey-reactive-feed.yaml` -- structured feed journey schema
- `journey-reactive-feed.feature` -- feed journey Gherkin (17 scenarios)
- `journey-agent-coordination-visual.md` -- coordination journey with flow diagrams
- `journey-agent-coordination.yaml` -- structured coordination journey schema
- `journey-agent-coordination.feature` -- coordination journey Gherkin (17 scenarios)
- `shared-artifacts-registry.md` -- 10 tracked artifacts with sources and consumers

1→# US-GRC-01: Live Governance Feed via SSE
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a workspace admin who monitors agent activity via the governance feed. He finds it frustrating to manually refresh the feed page 8-10 times per session to see current state. When an agent confirms a decision or the Observer creates a conflict observation, Marcus does not see it until the next page refresh -- which can be minutes later. During that gap, other agents may continue working with stale assumptions.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Workspace admin | Monitors governance feed in browser | Wants to see graph changes in real-time without manual refresh
     8→
     9→## Solution
    10→Replace the current poll-on-load governance feed with a persistent SSE stream that pushes graph changes to the browser as they happen. The initial feed state is loaded via the existing GET endpoint. Subsequent changes are delivered via SSE events that the client merges into the feed state.
    11→
    12→## Job Story Trace
    13→- JS-GRC-01: Real-Time Governance Awareness
    14→- Outcome #1: Minimize the time between a graph change and the workspace admin seeing it in the feed (Score: 16.5)
    15→- Outcome #5: Minimize the number of manual page refreshes needed to see current feed state (Score: 16.0)
    16→
    17→## Domain Examples
    18→
    19→### 1: Decision Confirmation Appears Live
    20→Marcus Oliveira has the governance feed open for workspace "montreal." The blocking tier shows 2 items including provisional decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs." At 3:43 PM, the chat agent confirms the decision via conversation C-12. Within 2 seconds, the decision disappears from the blocking tier and a new awareness item appears: "Decision confirmed: Standardize on tRPC for all APIs." The blocking count badge updates from 2 to 1. Marcus did not refresh the page.
    21→
    22→### 2: Observer Warning Surfaces Without Refresh
    23→Marcus is reviewing the feed at 10:15 AM. The Observer agent runs a graph scan and creates a warning observation: "Schema migration missing for new field on task table." Within 2 seconds, a new review item slides into the feed with severity "warning" and source "observer_agent." The review count increases from 3 to 4. Marcus sees the [NEW] badge and clicks to investigate.
    24→
    25→### 3: Connection Drops and Recovers
    26→Marcus's MacBook goes to sleep at 3:55 PM. The SSE connection drops. The feed header changes from "Connected [*] live" to "Reconnecting..." with an amber indicator. At 3:59 PM Marcus wakes the laptop. The SSE reconnects. The feed header shows "Reconnected. 3 updates received since 3:55 PM." Two new review items and one awareness item appear with [NEW] badges. Marcus can see exactly what changed while he was away.
    27→
    28→### 4: High-Volume Event Burst
    29→At 2:00 PM, Marcus imports a large batch of tasks via the API. This creates 50 new task records in 10 seconds. The feed does not receive 50 separate SSE events. Instead, the SSE bridge batches events within a 500ms window and delivers a single "feed_update" event with the count of new awareness items. The feed shows "+12 new awareness items" (only the first 12 that cross the feed item limit) without overwhelming the client.
    30→
    31→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    32→
    33→### Scenario: Feed establishes SSE connection on page load
    34→Given Marcus Oliveira navigates to the governance feed for workspace "montreal"
    35→When the feed page finishes loading
    36→Then an SSE connection is established to /api/workspaces/montreal/feed/stream
    37→And the connection indicator shows "Connected" with a live indicator
    38→And the initial feed state is loaded via GET /api/workspaces/montreal/feed
    39→
    40→### Scenario: Graph change appears in feed within 2 seconds
    41→Given Marcus has the governance feed open for workspace "montreal"
    42→And the feed shows 2 blocking items
    43→When the extraction pipeline creates a new provisional decision "Use event sourcing for audit trail" with confidence 0.92
    44→Then within 2 seconds a new blocking item appears in the feed
    45→And the blocking count updates to 3
    46→And the new item has a [NEW] visual indicator
    47→
    48→### Scenario: Item moves between tiers on status change
    49→Given Marcus has the governance feed open
    50→And the blocking tier contains provisional decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs"
    51→When chat_agent confirms the decision
    52→Then within 2 seconds the decision is removed from the blocking tier
    53→And a new awareness item appears with text "Decision confirmed: Standardize on tRPC for all APIs"
    54→And tier counts update accordingly
    55→
    56→### Scenario: SSE reconnects after network loss with delta sync
    57→Given Marcus has the governance feed open and last received event ID "evt-42"
    58→When the SSE connection drops for 30 seconds
    59→And during disconnection 1 observation and 1 task completion occur
    60→Then the connection indicator changes to "Reconnecting..."
    61→And when the connection recovers, the feed shows "Reconnected. 2 updates received"
    62→And the missed items appear in the correct tiers with [NEW] badges
    63→
    64→### Scenario: Extended disconnection triggers full refresh
    65→Given Marcus has the governance feed open
    66→When the SSE connection drops for 10 minutes
    67→Then on reconnection the server triggers a full feed refresh (not delta)
    68→And the feed state is completely rebuilt from the current graph state
    69→And the banner shows "Reconnected. Feed refreshed."
    70→
    71→### Scenario: SSE keep-alive maintains connection
    72→Given Marcus has the governance feed open with no graph changes for 5 minutes
    73→Then the SSE connection sends a keep-alive comment every 15 seconds
    74→And the connection indicator remains "Connected"
    75→And the connection does not time out
    76→
    77→## Acceptance Criteria
    78→- [ ] SSE endpoint GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream established on feed page load
    79→- [ ] Feed items delivered via SSE use the same GovernanceFeedItem contract as the GET endpoint
    80→- [ ] New items appear in the correct tier within 2 seconds of the graph write (95th percentile)
    81→- [ ] Connection status indicator accurately reflects EventSource readyState (Connected/Reconnecting/Disconnected)
    82→- [ ] On reconnection, missed events are replayed from the last event ID (delta sync)
    83→- [ ] After 10+ minutes disconnection, a full feed refresh is performed instead of delta
    84→- [ ] SSE keep-alive sent every 15 seconds to prevent connection timeout
    85→- [ ] High-volume event bursts are batched within a 500ms window to prevent client overload
    86→- [ ] No duplicate feed items after reconnection
    87→
    88→## Technical Notes
    89→- Depends on: SurrealDB LIVE SELECT capability (requires WebSocket connection from server to SurrealDB -- current transport is HTTP per AGENTS.md; this is a known constraint that needs resolution)
    90→- Depends on: existing SSE registry infrastructure (streaming/sse-registry.ts) -- needs extension from per-message to per-workspace scope
    91→- Depends on: existing GovernanceFeedItem contract (shared/contracts.ts)
    92→- Current feed route (feed/feed-route.ts) runs 14 parallel queries on each request; SSE bridge must avoid re-running all 14 queries on every graph change
    93→- SurrealDB LIVE SELECT requires ws:// or wss:// transport. Current SURREAL_URL uses HTTP. Transport change needed.
    94→- Constraint: LIVE SELECT cannot be used on high-volume tables (trace, message) without flooding the SSE bridge. Exclude these tables; use existing DEFINE EVENT webhooks for those.
    95→- Phase: 3 (Foundation)
    96→
    97→## Dependencies
    98→- Transport protocol change: SURREAL_URL from http:// to ws:// (or dual connection: HTTP for queries, WS for LIVE SELECT)
    99→- SSE registry refactor: extend from per-message to per-workspace scope

# US-GRC-01: Live Governance Feed via SSE

## Problem
Marcus Oliveira is a workspace admin who monitors agent activity via the governance feed. He finds it frustrating to manually refresh the feed page 8-10 times per session to see current state. When an agent confirms a decision or the Observer creates a conflict observation, Marcus does not see it until the next page refresh -- which can be minutes later. During that gap, other agents may continue working with stale assumptions.

## Who
- Workspace admin | Monitors governance feed in browser | Wants to see graph changes in real-time without manual refresh

## Solution
Replace the current poll-on-load governance feed with a persistent SSE stream that pushes graph changes to the browser as they happen. The initial feed state is loaded via the existing GET endpoint. Subsequent changes are delivered via SSE events that the client merges into the feed state.

## Job Story Trace
- JS-GRC-01: Real-Time Governance Awareness
- Outcome #1: Minimize the time between a graph change and the workspace admin seeing it in the feed (Score: 16.5)
- Outcome #5: Minimize the number of manual page refreshes needed to see current feed state (Score: 16.0)

## Domain Examples

### 1: Decision Confirmation Appears Live
Marcus Oliveira has the governance feed open for workspace "montreal." The blocking tier shows 2 items including provisional decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs." At 3:43 PM, the chat agent confirms the decision via conversation C-12. Within 2 seconds, the decision disappears from the blocking tier and a new awareness item appears: "Decision confirmed: Standardize on tRPC for all APIs." The blocking count badge updates from 2 to 1. Marcus did not refresh the page.

### 2: Observer Warning Surfaces Without Refresh
Marcus is reviewing the feed at 10:15 AM. The Observer agent runs a graph scan and creates a warning observation: "Schema migration missing for new field on task table." Within 2 seconds, a new review item slides into the feed with severity "warning" and source "observer_agent." The review count increases from 3 to 4. Marcus sees the [NEW] badge and clicks to investigate.

### 3: Connection Drops and Recovers
Marcus's MacBook goes to sleep at 3:55 PM. The SSE connection drops. The feed header changes from "Connected [*] live" to "Reconnecting..." with an amber indicator. At 3:59 PM Marcus wakes the laptop. The SSE reconnects. The feed header shows "Reconnected. 3 updates received since 3:55 PM." Two new review items and one awareness item appear with [NEW] badges. Marcus can see exactly what changed while he was away.

### 4: High-Volume Event Burst
At 2:00 PM, Marcus imports a large batch of tasks via the API. This creates 50 new task records in 10 seconds. The feed does not receive 50 separate SSE events. Instead, the SSE bridge batches events within a 500ms window and delivers a single "feed_update" event with the count of new awareness items. The feed shows "+12 new awareness items" (only the first 12 that cross the feed item limit) without overwhelming the client.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Feed establishes SSE connection on page load
Given Marcus Oliveira navigates to the governance feed for workspace "montreal"
When the feed page finishes loading
Then an SSE connection is established to /api/workspaces/montreal/feed/stream
And the connection indicator shows "Connected" with a live indicator
And the initial feed state is loaded via GET /api/workspaces/montreal/feed

### Scenario: Graph change appears in feed within 2 seconds
Given Marcus has the governance feed open for workspace "montreal"
And the feed shows 2 blocking items
When the extraction pipeline creates a new provisional decision "Use event sourcing for audit trail" with confidence 0.92
Then within 2 seconds a new blocking item appears in the feed
And the blocking count updates to 3
And the new item has a [NEW] visual indicator

### Scenario: Item moves between tiers on status change
Given Marcus has the governance feed open
And the blocking tier contains provisional decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs"
When chat_agent confirms the decision
Then within 2 seconds the decision is removed from the blocking tier
And a new awareness item appears with text "Decision confirmed: Standardize on tRPC for all APIs"
And tier counts update accordingly

### Scenario: SSE reconnects after network loss with delta sync
Given Marcus has the governance feed open and last received event ID "evt-42"
When the SSE connection drops for 30 seconds
And during disconnection 1 observation and 1 task completion occur
Then the connection indicator changes to "Reconnecting..."
And when the connection recovers, the feed shows "Reconnected. 2 updates received"
And the missed items appear in the correct tiers with [NEW] badges

### Scenario: Extended disconnection triggers full refresh
Given Marcus has the governance feed open
When the SSE connection drops for 10 minutes
Then on reconnection the server triggers a full feed refresh (not delta)
And the feed state is completely rebuilt from the current graph state
And the banner shows "Reconnected. Feed refreshed."

### Scenario: SSE keep-alive maintains connection
Given Marcus has the governance feed open with no graph changes for 5 minutes
Then the SSE connection sends a keep-alive comment every 15 seconds
And the connection indicator remains "Connected"
And the connection does not time out

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSE endpoint GET /api/workspaces/:id/feed/stream established on feed page load
- [ ] Feed items delivered via SSE use the same GovernanceFeedItem contract as the GET endpoint
- [ ] New items appear in the correct tier within 2 seconds of the graph write (95th percentile)
- [ ] Connection status indicator accurately reflects EventSource readyState (Connected/Reconnecting/Disconnected)
- [ ] On reconnection, missed events are replayed from the last event ID (delta sync)
- [ ] After 10+ minutes disconnection, a full feed refresh is performed instead of delta
- [ ] SSE keep-alive sent every 15 seconds to prevent connection timeout
- [ ] High-volume event bursts are batched within a 500ms window to prevent client overload
- [ ] No duplicate feed items after reconnection

## Technical Notes
- Depends on: SurrealDB LIVE SELECT capability (requires WebSocket connection from server to SurrealDB -- current transport is HTTP per AGENTS.md; this is a known constraint that needs resolution)
- Depends on: existing SSE registry infrastructure (streaming/sse-registry.ts) -- needs extension from per-message to per-workspace scope
- Depends on: existing GovernanceFeedItem contract (shared/contracts.ts)
- Current feed route (feed/feed-route.ts) runs 14 parallel queries on each request; SSE bridge must avoid re-running all 14 queries on every graph change
- SurrealDB LIVE SELECT requires ws:// or wss:// transport. Current SURREAL_URL uses HTTP. Transport change needed.
- Constraint: LIVE SELECT cannot be used on high-volume tables (trace, message) without flooding the SSE bridge. Exclude these tables; use existing DEFINE EVENT webhooks for those.
- Phase: 3 (Foundation)

## Dependencies
- Transport protocol change: SURREAL_URL from http:// to ws:// (or dual connection: HTTP for queries, WS for LIVE SELECT)
- SSE registry refactor: extend from per-message to per-workspace scope
- GovernanceFeedItem type must be shared between feed-route.ts GET handler and SSE bridge

1→# US-GRC-02: Graph Event Classifier
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a workspace admin who is overwhelmed when all graph changes are treated equally. A new info-severity observation about code style should not demand the same attention as a conflict observation that invalidates an agent's active work. Currently, the Observer webhook (DEFINE EVENT) fires for specific transitions but there is no unified classification of "how important is this change and who needs to know."
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Workspace admin | Monitors governance feed | Wants noise filtered: important changes highlighted, routine changes quiet
     8→- Agent Coordinator (Phase 4) | Routes notifications | Needs deterministic classification to decide interrupt vs enqueue vs log
     9→
    10→## Solution
    11→Build a deterministic event classifier that receives LIVE SELECT events and produces notification-level decisions (interrupt / enqueue / log) per target (feed, specific agent, observer). Classification rules are based on entity type, transition type, severity, and dependency context. No LLM in the classification path -- purely rule-based for predictability and speed.
    12→
    13→## Job Story Trace
    14→- JS-GRC-01: Real-Time Governance Awareness (notification level determines feed tier)
    15→- JS-GRC-02: Reactive Agent Wake-Up (notification level determines agent delivery)
    16→- Outcome #8: Maximize the likelihood that notification urgency matches the actual impact of the change (Score: 14.9)
    17→
    18→## Domain Examples
    19→
    20→### 1: Decision Confirmation -- Multi-Target Classification
    21→At 3:43 PM, decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs" (decision:d-99) transitions from "provisional" to "confirmed" in workspace "montreal." The event classifier receives the LIVE SELECT event and produces:
    22→- Target: feed -> level: log (decision status change is feed-worthy)
    23→- Target: observer -> level: log (triggers verification against active tasks)
    24→- Target: agent_session:s-88 (Agent B, active on task:t-47 which depends on decision:d-99) -> level: enqueue
    25→
    26→### 2: Decision Superseded -- Interrupt Level
    27→At 4:10 PM, decision "Use REST for billing API" (decision:d-55) transitions from "confirmed" to "superseded." Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-55. The classifier produces:
    28→- Target: feed -> level: log
    29→- Target: agent_session:s-88 -> level: interrupt (dependency superseded, active work may be invalid)
    30→
    31→### 3: Info Observation -- Log Only
    32→At 11:00 AM, the coding agent creates an info-severity observation "Unused import in auth module." The classifier produces:
    33→- Target: feed -> level: log
    34→No agent notifications. No observer trigger. Minimal noise.
    35→
    36→### 4: Conflict Observation -- Interrupt for Affected Agent
    37→At 2:30 PM, the Observer creates a conflict-severity observation "Task T-47 contradicts confirmed decision D-99" targeting task:t-47. Agent B has an active session on task:t-47. The classifier produces:
    38→- Target: feed -> level: log
    39→- Target: agent_session:s-88 -> level: interrupt (conflict directly targets agent's active task)
    40→
    41→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    42→
    43→### Scenario: Decision confirmation classified correctly
    44→Given workspace "montreal" has LIVE SELECT active on the decision table
    45→And Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-99
    46→When decision:d-99 transitions from "provisional" to "confirmed"
    47→Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for target "feed"
    48→And notification level "log" for target "observer"
    49→And notification level "enqueue" for target agent_session:s-88
    50→
    51→### Scenario: Decision superseded triggers interrupt
    52→Given Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-55
    53→When decision:d-55 transitions from "confirmed" to "superseded"
    54→Then the classifier produces notification level "interrupt" for agent_session:s-88
    55→
    56→### Scenario: Info observation produces log only
    57→Given the coding agent creates an observation with severity "info"
    58→When the event classifier processes the observation creation event
    59→Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for target "feed"
    60→And produces no agent-targeted notifications
    61→
    62→### Scenario: Conflict observation interrupts affected agent
    63→Given Agent B has an active session on task:t-47
    64→And the Observer creates a conflict-severity observation targeting task:t-47
    65→When the event classifier processes the observation creation event
    66→Then the classifier produces notification level "interrupt" for agent_session:s-88
    67→
    68→### Scenario: Task completion enqueues for dependent agents
    69→Given Agent C has an active session on task:t-60
    70→And task:t-60 has a depends_on edge to task:t-47
    71→When task:t-47 transitions from "in_progress" to "done"
    72→Then the classifier produces notification level "enqueue" for Agent C's session
    73→
    74→### Scenario: Agent session end triggers observer and dependents
    75→Given Agent B ends session on task:t-47 in workspace "montreal"
    76→And task:t-102 depends_on task:t-47
    77→And Agent D has an active session on task:t-102
    78→When the agent_session ended event fires
    79→Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for observer (trace analysis)
    80→And notification level "enqueue" for Agent D's session
    81→
    82→## Acceptance Criteria
    83→- [ ] Classifier is deterministic: same input always produces same output (no LLM, no randomness)
    84→- [ ] Classifier handles all entity types: decision, task, observation, question, agent_session, suggestion, learning
    85→- [ ] Three notification levels supported: interrupt, enqueue, log
    86→- [ ] Classifier resolves dependencies via graph traversal to determine agent-level notifications
    87→- [ ] Classification completes within 100ms for typical graph topologies (< 1000 entities)
    88→- [ ] Classification rules are documented and auditable
    89→- [ ] Unknown entity types default to "log" level (no crashes on unexpected tables)
    90→
    91→## Technical Notes
    92→- Classifier must NOT use LLM -- deterministic rules only for predictability and speed
    93→- Graph traversal for dependency resolution needs: depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to edges
    94→- Active session lookup: `SELECT * FROM agent_session WHERE task_id IN $affected_tasks AND orchestrator_status = "active"`
    95→- Must handle the case where LIVE SELECT delivers events for entities without a workspace field (e.g., relation tables) -- skip classification or resolve workspace from connected entity
    96→- Current DEFINE EVENT webhooks (observer-route.ts SUPPORTED_TABLES) continue to work alongside the new classifier -- they are not replaced, they are complemented
    97→- Phase: 3 (Foundation -- needed by both feed SSE bridge and agent coordinator)
    98→
    99→## Dependencies
   100→- US-GRC-01 (LIVE SELECT -> SSE bridge) provides the events that the classifier processes
   101→- Graph traversal queries must be written to work with existing schema (depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to edges)
   102→

# US-GRC-02: Graph Event Classifier

## Problem
Marcus Oliveira is a workspace admin who is overwhelmed when all graph changes are treated equally. A new info-severity observation about code style should not demand the same attention as a conflict observation that invalidates an agent's active work. Currently, the Observer webhook (DEFINE EVENT) fires for specific transitions but there is no unified classification of "how important is this change and who needs to know."

## Who
- Workspace admin | Monitors governance feed | Wants noise filtered: important changes highlighted, routine changes quiet
- Agent Coordinator (Phase 4) | Routes notifications | Needs deterministic classification to decide interrupt vs enqueue vs log

## Solution
Build a deterministic event classifier that receives LIVE SELECT events and produces notification-level decisions (interrupt / enqueue / log) per target (feed, specific agent, observer). Classification rules are based on entity type, transition type, severity, and dependency context. No LLM in the classification path -- purely rule-based for predictability and speed.

## Job Story Trace
- JS-GRC-01: Real-Time Governance Awareness (notification level determines feed tier)
- JS-GRC-02: Reactive Agent Wake-Up (notification level determines agent delivery)
- Outcome #8: Maximize the likelihood that notification urgency matches the actual impact of the change (Score: 14.9)

## Domain Examples

### 1: Decision Confirmation -- Multi-Target Classification
At 3:43 PM, decision "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs" (decision:d-99) transitions from "provisional" to "confirmed" in workspace "montreal." The event classifier receives the LIVE SELECT event and produces:
- Target: feed -> level: log (decision status change is feed-worthy)
- Target: observer -> level: log (triggers verification against active tasks)
- Target: agent_session:s-88 (Agent B, active on task:t-47 which depends on decision:d-99) -> level: enqueue

### 2: Decision Superseded -- Interrupt Level
At 4:10 PM, decision "Use REST for billing API" (decision:d-55) transitions from "confirmed" to "superseded." Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-55. The classifier produces:
- Target: feed -> level: log
- Target: agent_session:s-88 -> level: interrupt (dependency superseded, active work may be invalid)

### 3: Info Observation -- Log Only
At 11:00 AM, the coding agent creates an info-severity observation "Unused import in auth module." The classifier produces:
- Target: feed -> level: log
No agent notifications. No observer trigger. Minimal noise.

### 4: Conflict Observation -- Interrupt for Affected Agent
At 2:30 PM, the Observer creates a conflict-severity observation "Task T-47 contradicts confirmed decision D-99" targeting task:t-47. Agent B has an active session on task:t-47. The classifier produces:
- Target: feed -> level: log
- Target: agent_session:s-88 -> level: interrupt (conflict directly targets agent's active task)

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Decision confirmation classified correctly
Given workspace "montreal" has LIVE SELECT active on the decision table
And Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-99
When decision:d-99 transitions from "provisional" to "confirmed"
Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for target "feed"
And notification level "log" for target "observer"
And notification level "enqueue" for target agent_session:s-88

### Scenario: Decision superseded triggers interrupt
Given Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-55
When decision:d-55 transitions from "confirmed" to "superseded"
Then the classifier produces notification level "interrupt" for agent_session:s-88

### Scenario: Info observation produces log only
Given the coding agent creates an observation with severity "info"
When the event classifier processes the observation creation event
Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for target "feed"
And produces no agent-targeted notifications

### Scenario: Conflict observation interrupts affected agent
Given Agent B has an active session on task:t-47
And the Observer creates a conflict-severity observation targeting task:t-47
When the event classifier processes the observation creation event
Then the classifier produces notification level "interrupt" for agent_session:s-88

### Scenario: Task completion enqueues for dependent agents
Given Agent C has an active session on task:t-60
And task:t-60 has a depends_on edge to task:t-47
When task:t-47 transitions from "in_progress" to "done"
Then the classifier produces notification level "enqueue" for Agent C's session

### Scenario: Agent session end triggers observer and dependents
Given Agent B ends session on task:t-47 in workspace "montreal"
And task:t-102 depends_on task:t-47
And Agent D has an active session on task:t-102
When the agent_session ended event fires
Then the classifier produces notification level "log" for observer (trace analysis)
And notification level "enqueue" for Agent D's session

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Classifier is deterministic: same input always produces same output (no LLM, no randomness)
- [ ] Classifier handles all entity types: decision, task, observation, question, agent_session, suggestion, learning
- [ ] Three notification levels supported: interrupt, enqueue, log
- [ ] Classifier resolves dependencies via graph traversal to determine agent-level notifications
- [ ] Classification completes within 100ms for typical graph topologies (< 1000 entities)
- [ ] Classification rules are documented and auditable
- [ ] Unknown entity types default to "log" level (no crashes on unexpected tables)

## Technical Notes
- Classifier must NOT use LLM -- deterministic rules only for predictability and speed
- Graph traversal for dependency resolution needs: depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to edges
- Active session lookup: `SELECT * FROM agent_session WHERE task_id IN $affected_tasks AND orchestrator_status = "active"`
- Must handle the case where LIVE SELECT delivers events for entities without a workspace field (e.g., relation tables) -- skip classification or resolve workspace from connected entity
- Current DEFINE EVENT webhooks (observer-route.ts SUPPORTED_TABLES) continue to work alongside the new classifier -- they are not replaced, they are complemented
- Phase: 3 (Foundation -- needed by both feed SSE bridge and agent coordinator)

## Dependencies
- US-GRC-01 (LIVE SELECT -> SSE bridge) provides the events that the classifier processes
- Graph traversal queries must be written to work with existing schema (depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to edges)

content.content:
1→# US-GRC-03: Agent Coordinator with Context Injection
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Marcus Oliveira is a workspace admin who currently acts as the integration layer between agents. When Agent A confirms a decision, Agent B (working on a dependent task) continues with stale assumptions for minutes until Marcus manually notices the inconsistency and re-runs Agent B's work. Marcus spends significant time relaying context between agents that should be coordinating through the graph automatically.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Workspace admin | Coordinates agent work | Wants agents to self-coordinate without human relay
     8→- Chat Agent (orchestrator) | Dispatches subagents | Needs subagents to have current context
     9→- Coding agents (MCP) | Work on assigned tasks | Need to know when task dependencies change mid-session
    10→
    11→## Solution
    12→Build an Agent Coordinator as a standalone always-on service (started with the server) that subscribes to classified events from the Event Classifier, resolves affected agents via graph traversal, and writes context updates to a `context_queue` table in SurrealDB. The Coordinator does NOT inject context itself — the LLM proxy reads from `context_queue` during its existing context injection pipeline (`context-injector.ts`) and delivers updates when the agent makes its next API call. Include loop dampening to prevent cascading notification storms.
    13→
    14→### Architecture Split
    15→- **Coordinator** (this story): LIVE SELECT listener → graph traversal → write to `context_queue` table
    16→- **Delivery** (US-GRC-04, proxy): proxy reads `context_queue` for current session → injects into system prompt → marks delivered
    17→
    18→This split exists because the Coordinator must be always-on (listening to graph events between requests), while the proxy only runs on API calls. The `context_queue` table is the interface between them.
    19→
    20→## Job Story Trace
    21→- JS-GRC-02: Reactive Agent Wake-Up
    22→- JS-GRC-03: Real-Time Conflict Detection
    23→- Outcome #2: Minimize the likelihood of an agent working with stale context after another agent changes a dependency (Score: 17.4)
    24→
    25→## Domain Examples
    26→
    27→### 1: Enqueue -- Decision Confirmation Context Update
    28→Agent B (session:s-88) is working on task "Migrate billing API" (task:t-47), which depends_on decision:d-99 "API transport standard." At 3:43 PM, the chat agent confirms decision:d-99 with value "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs." The Event Classifier produces level "enqueue" for session:s-88. The Coordinator enqueues a context update:
    29→
    30→```
    31→[CONTEXT UPDATE - received during active session]
    32→Decision confirmed: "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs" (decision:d-99)
    33→Confirmed by: chat_agent via conversation C-12
    34→Impact: Your task "Migrate billing API" (task:t-47) depends on this decision.
    35→Action: Review your current approach against the confirmed decision.
    36→```
    37→
    38→When Agent B makes its next tool call (search_entities), the context update is prepended to the system context. Agent B acknowledges and adjusts its approach.
    39→
    40→### 2: Interrupt -- Decision Superseded (Phase 5)
    41→At 4:10 PM, decision:d-55 "Use REST for billing API" is superseded by decision:d-99 "Standardize on tRPC." Agent B has an active session on task:t-47 which depends_on decision:d-55. The classifier produces level "interrupt." The Coordinator injects:
    42→
    43→```
    44→[URGENT CONTEXT UPDATE - dependency invalidated]
    45→Decision SUPERSEDED: "Use REST for billing API" (decision:d-55)
    46→Superseded by: "Standardize on tRPC for all APIs" (decision:d-99)
    47→Impact: Task "Migrate billing API" (task:t-47) depends on the superseded decision.
    48→Recommendation: Pause current approach. Re-evaluate against the new decision.
    49→```
    50→
    51→Agent B's current response completes normally. On the next turn, the urgent context is injected.
    52→
    53→### 3: Loop Dampening -- Cascading Observations
    54→The Observer agent creates obs-1 targeting task:t-47. Peer review creates obs-2. Peer review of obs-2 creates obs-3. All within 45 seconds. The dampener detects 3 events from observer_agent on task:t-47 in 60 seconds. A fourth event is downgraded to "log" only. A meta-observation is created: "Cascading event loop dampened on task:t-47 (3 events in 45s from observer_agent)." This appears in Marcus's feed as a review item.
    55→
    56→### 4: No Active Session -- Skip Agent Notification
    57→Agent C completed its session on task:t-102 two hours ago. Task:t-102 depends_on decision:d-99. When decision:d-99 is confirmed, the Coordinator finds no active session for task:t-102 and does not generate a notification. The change is still visible in the feed (log level) and will be part of the context for Agent C's next session via the regular MCP context loader.
    58→
    59→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    60→
    61→### Scenario: Coordinator enqueues context update for affected agent
    62→Given Agent B has an active session (s-88) on task "Migrate billing API" (task:t-47)
    63→And task:t-47 has a depends_on edge to decision:d-99
    64→When the Event Classifier produces level "enqueue" for session:s-88 with decision:d-99 confirmation context
    65→Then the Coordinator stores a context update in session:s-88's queue
    66→And the update includes entity reference, change description, and impact on task:t-47
    67→
    68→### Scenario: Agent reads enqueued context on next tool turn
    69→Given Agent B's session queue contains 1 context update about decision:d-99
    70→When Agent B makes its next tool call
    71→Then the context update is prepended to Agent B's system context
    72→And the update is labeled "[CONTEXT UPDATE - received during active session]"
    73→And Agent B's tool call proceeds normally
    74→
    75→### Scenario: Multiple updates batched into single injection
    76→Given Agent B's session queue contains 3 context updates accumulated over 15 seconds
    77→When Agent B makes its next tool call
    78→Then all 3 updates are delivered in a single injection block
    79→And updates are ordered chronologically
    80→And the block is labeled "[3 CONTEXT UPDATES - received during active session]"
    81→
    82→### Scenario: Stale queue items discarded after TTL
    83→Given a context update was enqueued for Agent B at 10:00 AM
    84→And the queue TTL is 30 minutes
    85→When Agent B makes a tool call at 10:45 AM
    86→Then the update is not injected (expired)
    87→And a log entry records "context update discarded: TTL expired"
    88→
    89→### Scenario: Coordinator skips agents without active sessions
    90→Given Agent C completed its session on task:t-102 two hours ago
    91→And task:t-102 depends_on decision:d-99
    92→When the Coordinator processes a change event for decision:d-99
    93→Then no notification is generated for Agent C
    94→And the change is routed to feed only
    95→
    96→### Scenario: Loop dampening activates after threshold
    97→Given the Observer creates obs-1, obs-2, obs-3 all targeting task:t-47 within 45 seconds
    98→When obs-4 targeting task:t-47 triggers the event classifier
    99→Then the dampener forces notification level to "log" for task:t-47 events
   100→And a meta-observation is created: "Cascading event loop dampened on task:t-47"
   101→And the meta-observation is visible in Marcus's governance feed
   102→
   103→### Scenario: Dampening resets after window expires
   104→Given dampening was activated on task:t-47 at 10:01:00
   105→When a new event for task:t-47 arrives at 10:02:05 (65 seconds later)
   106→Then the dampener has reset
   107→And the event is classified normally
   108→
   109→## Acceptance Criteria
   110→- [ ] Coordinator resolves affected agents via graph traversal (depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to edges)
   111→- [ ] Only agents with active sessions (orchestrator_status = "active") receive notifications
   112→- [ ] Enqueue: context update stored in per-session queue, delivered on next tool-use turn
   113→- [ ] Queue items have configurable TTL (default: 30 minutes), stale items discarded
   114→- [ ] Multiple queued updates are batched into a single injection with chronological ordering
   115→- [ ] Context injection is clearly framed as mid-session update (distinct from original task context)
   116→- [ ] Loop dampening: >3 events on same entity from same source in 60 seconds triggers dampening
   117→- [ ] Dampened events are logged but do not trigger agent notifications
   118→- [ ] Meta-observation created when dampening activates (visible in feed)
   119→- [ ] Graph traversal completes within 200ms for workspaces with < 1000 entities
   120→
   121→## Technical Notes
   122→- **`context_queue` table** (new, SCHEMAFULL): `session` (record<agent_session>), `level` ("enqueue" | "interrupt"), `payload` (object: entity ref, change description, impact, recommendation), `created_at` (datetime), `delivered_at` (option<datetime>), `expired_at` (option<datetime>). TTL: 30 minutes (expired items ignored by proxy, cleaned up periodically).
   123→- **Coordinator is always-on**: started in `start-server.ts` alongside the HTTP server. Subscribes to LIVE SELECT on classified event tables. Runs graph traversal on each event to find affected sessions.
   124→- **Proxy reads, Coordinator writes**: The proxy's `context-injector.ts` gains a `loadPendingContextUpdates(sessionId)` step that queries `context_queue WHERE session = $sess AND delivered_at IS NONE AND created_at > time::now() - 30m ORDER BY created_at`. After injection, marks items `delivered_at = time::now()`.
   125→- Loop dampening state is per-workspace, per-entity, per-source-agent. Implemented as in-memory sliding window counter in the Coordinator process.
   126→- The Coordinator does NOT cancel agent generations. It only writes to the queue. Delivery timing is controlled by when the agent next calls the proxy.
   127→- Graph traversal queries are edge-based (depends_on, relates_to, belongs_to), not vector-based — no KNN + WHERE bug concern.
   128→- Phase: 4 (Coordinator -- depends on Phase 3 foundation)
   129→
   130→## Dependencies
   131→- US-GRC-01: LIVE SELECT → SSE bridge (provides the event stream infrastructure)
   132→- US-GRC-02: Event Classifier (provides classified events with notification levels)
   133→- Existing agent session lifecycle (orchestrator/session-lifecycle.ts)
   134→- Existing proxy context injection pipeline (proxy/context-injector.ts) — delivery handled by US-GRC-04
